system.damage: cudgel d8 (close, crude), snapping tongue d6 (reach), poison blowgun needles d6 (near)
system.specialQuality: excellent night vision, salt burns their skin

selected: to bring home captives for sacrifice

system.moves:
- Surround their quarry unnoticed
- Leap a shocking distance
- Club someone senseless on the head
- Tie a fast knot

They’re tall as a short man, hunched on too-long legs and too-long arms. A toad- like head juts forward from broad shoul- ders, the eyes entirely too big, the mouth entirely too wide. They’re covered in moist scales and horn-like growths, and draped in belts and bandoliers of woven grass. These are the suarachan most often en- countered, the ones that raid Marshedge and the ones that fen-walkers most fear. Truly, they spend more time fishing and hunting than seeking sacrifices, and they’re just as happy to claim a deer as a man. But deer are harder to catch, on the whole.
